attributes.role: Owns invoices, payment follow-up, receipts, and open balance exceptions.
Requests service, confirms expectations, pays, and gives feedback.
Owns intake, scheduling, customer updates, and review requests.
Turns scheduled work into technician-ready route assignments.
Keeps technician trucks stocked and records material exceptions.
Reviews operating evidence and resolves cross-capability gaps.
Owns quality recovery, priority rules, and daily capacity tradeoffs.
Completes work, records job evidence, and communicates field status.

attributes.outcome: Completed work turns into collected cash or a named open balance with an accountable owner.
Technicians arrive prepared, complete the promised work, and produce enough evidence to close the work order.
Technician capacity, skills, and truck materials are ready before customer commitments are made.
Customers know what is happening, can recover from service issues, and are asked for feedback at the right time.
Customers receive a confirmed visit window with enough context for the business to dispatch the right technician.

nodes.label: Emergency priority rules need owner definition
Completion photo standard is not defined
Truck stock min/max rules need owner review

attributes.target: Most completed jobs are collected before the next business day.
Same-day or next-business-day window is visible before confirmation.
Service issues receive owner response before the next service day.
Daily capacity and critical truck stock are reviewed before dispatch.
Completed visits close or escalate before end of service day.

attributes.contract: service job identity links workflow evidence

attributes.purpose: Customer record, service category, appointment window, and job status.
Confirmations, arrival updates, issue recovery, and review requests.
Truck stock, parts usage, replenishment, and low-stock exceptions.
Technician checklist, photos, parts, signatures, and completion notes.
Invoices, payment links, receipts, refunds, and aging status.
Review requests, customer satisfaction signals, and follow-up status.
Technician assignments, geography, arrival estimates, and route load.
Skill, license, territory, capacity, and PTO constraints.
